sections.name: 1. Schema Conformance
checks: architecture.headlinePurpose is a non-empty string
architecture.readerJourney is an array of ≥ 3 questions
architecture.sections is an array with ≥ 3 items
Every section has id, question, objective, module, visual, narrativeStage
Every section.module is one of the 19 enum values
Every section.question ends with ?
Every section.narrativeStage is one of the 8 enum values
architecture.visualPlan is an array with ≥ 1 item
Every visualPlan item has sectionId, type (10 enum), purpose
architecture.complexity is one of the 5 enum values
architecture.estimatedReadingTime is a positive integer
architecture.templateUsed is one of the 5 enum values

sections.name: 2. Story Complexity (Step 1)
checks: Complexity is explicitly determined, not defaulted
Simple: ≤ 3 sections, ≤ 5 min read
Standard: 4–6 sections, 5–10 min read
Complex: 7–10 sections, 10–15 min read
Deep Investigation: 10+ sections, 15+ min read

sections.name: 3. Reader Journey (Step 2)
checks: Reader journey is mapped as questions, not section names
Questions follow logical sequence (what → why → how → what next)
Every question in the journey maps to a section

sections.name: 4. Section Objectives (Step 4)
checks: Every section answers exactly ONE question
No section has two questions in its objective
No question is shared between two sections

sections.name: 5. Visual Planning (Step 5)
checks: Every section has at least one visual element
No section with text-only content
Visual type matches section purpose (chart for data, timeline for chronology, etc.)
visualPlan covers all sections

sections.name: 6. Information Density (Step 6)
checks: Sections follow progressive disclosure (simple → deep)
Hero or Quick Facts comes first
Sources or Glossary comes last

sections.name: 7. Narrative Flow (Step 7)
checks: Sections follow: attention → understanding → evidence → context → analysis → consequences → solutions → future
No narrative stage is skipped unless justified
No stage appears out of order

sections.name: 8. Reader Questions (Step 8)
checks: Every section title is a question
Questions are distinct (no duplicates)
Questions are answerable by available evidence

sections.name: 9. Knowledge Connections (Step 9)
checks: ≥ 3 related items recommended
Connections cover at least 3 different types (stories, people, orgs, laws, schemes, budgets, countries)
Entity references resolve to existing entity IDs

sections.name: 10. Template Selection
checks: Template is one of the 5 options (breaking-news, explainer, investigation, data-story, the-fix)
Template selection is justified by story type and complexity
Template was customized (not used as-is)

sections.name: 11. Quality Gate
checks: Story complexity is explicitly determined
Reader journey is mapped in questions
Every section answers exactly ONE question
Every section has at least one visual element
Information density follows progressive disclosure
Narrative flow follows the 8-stage sequence
Every section title is a question
Knowledge connections recommend ≥ 3 related items
Template selection is justified
Estimated reading time is calculated from section count and complexity
Never start writing — design first
